key_insights.insight: Immigrant backgrounds can fuel entrepreneurship due to a sense of unfairness and a drive to improve their family's situation.
key_insights.quote: I do think that that kind of setting of, you know, having a lot of the intellectual capacity and all these things and then the kind of prerequisites potentially to do something different. And at the same time, this kind of drive of like, you kind of almost feel like it's unfair.

key_insights.insight: Society needs to show potential pathways to immigrant communities, channeling frustration and energy into productive avenues.
key_insights.quote: I wish that society would realize that like,there's going to be a lot of frustration, a lot of people with like, you know, energy,they want something different...And then it's just society's ability to try to showcase that that energy can be used to become Zlatan Ibrahimović.

key_insights.insight: True learning comes from experience and doing, not just passive listening.
key_insights.quote: As much as we think that learning is sitting in a room and listening to somebody, that is, you know, a very inefficient way of learning. We learn by doing, uh, by doing things ourselves.

key_insights.insight: It's critical to know 'what good looks like' before judging someone's work, requiring external perspectives and comparison.
key_insights.quote: The really tricky thing is to know what good looks like...what would great look work work look like and it's when you've established that understanding...then your job becomes so much easier.

key_insights.insight: Competition can be a catalyst for improvement, forcing a company to focus and innovate.
key_insights.quote: Klana in the last three years thanks to the competition with afterpay in the u.s has become such a much better company it has helped us so much to improve to get focused

key_insights.insight: Don't be afraid to say your company is not for everyone, to focus on attracting individuals that fit your mission.
key_insights.quote: It took us some time to dare to saythat Klana is not for everyone,that Klana is actually a companythat wants to attract peoplethat want to make a real impact,make a real difference, that want to learn,that want to be challenged.

key_insights.insight: Rules should be challenged, they cannot be an excuse for not thinking for yourself; there must be mechanisms to report when rules don't apply.
key_insights.quote: Rules can never be an excuse for not thinking for yourself...There's going to be exceptions in a large company.There has to be exceptionsbecause those are healthy signs of the factthat people are thinking for themselvesand judging by themselvesand not just hiding behind the rules.

key_insights.insight: It's important to have objective outsiders assess how employees are working in a company; not knowing what you don't know is a business curse.
key_insights.quote: I really also should have hadan objective outsider come in and do an assessmenton how he was working, how I was workingand everything in between.I think entrepreneurs don't do that.They, I think because they don't know what they don't know.

key_insights.insight: It is important to foster resilience in children by not removing all resistance.
key_insights.quote: I really want them to get the resistance.I want them to get to know themselvesand get to know that they can actually fend for themselves, that they can solve these problems for themselves. I don't want them to be without resistance.

actionable_advice.advice: If you're managing a role that you don't understand, sit next to the person doing it for a half day or day to learn what they do and build empathy.
actionable_advice.how_to: Sit down next to them, spend half a day, spend a day. Just look at when they're doing it, a designer or whatever it is, something that you don't know how to do yourself. Just sit next to them, see them do it.

actionable_advice.advice: When starting a company with friends, explicitly discuss and agree on expected hours and commitment levels to avoid future resentment.
actionable_advice.how_to: Sit down and ask, like, how many hours per week are you going to spend on this versus because you love exercisingand you love, you know, hanging out with your friends and so forth. Just so like, not that, you know, you can do it on 30 hours a weekor you could do it on 80, but just so you're can't be too big disalignment.

actionable_advice.advice: To avoid following bad advice, carefully analyze whether your advisors have really created great things themselves or were only part of a successful thing.
actionable_advice.how_to: Be careful with who you're listening to.Have they really contributed to success?Have they really built success?Or have they simply been in a company that was successful?

memorable_stories: Sebastian and his co-founder traveled the world without flying, sleeping in the streets and spending $10 a day.
Sebastian had a 30 second pitch to business angels at a Christmas event in an incubator and almost immediately, Jane Valerud approached him and invested in him.
Sebastian recalls a time when the Klarna team were on a round-the-world trip and arrived late to their ship due to a scheduling error and were stuck in Brisbane with no money.
contrarian_claims: The pursuit of stability may be inherently destabilizing; chaos might be a form of stability for some individuals.
Giving wealth to children may not make them happy and could create unintended pressures; Sebastian plans to give his children nothing.
Leaders should not always prescribe everything; they should provoke you to try to learn things yourselves.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Klarna Founder: From $0 to $46 Billion: Sebastian Siemiatkowski
